heading: Prerequisites
body:
To enable connections to Atlas using private endpoints, you must:

- Have a valid payment method already configured for your organization.

* Configure the private endpoint in the same region as the Atlas
  cluster that you want to connect to.

* Have either the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) or
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role in Atlas.

* Have an AWS (Amazon Web Services) user account with an IAM (Identity and Access Management) user policy that
  grants permissions to create, modify, describe, and delete
  endpoints. For more information on controlling the use of
  interface endpoints, see the [AWS Documentation](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-endpoints.html).

* **(Recommended)**:
  [Install the AWS CLI](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-install.html).

* If you have not already done so, create your VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) and EC2
  instances in AWS (Amazon Web Services). See the [AWS documentation](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/index.html) for guidance.

- Have either the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) or
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role in Atlas.

- Install the [Azure CLI](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azurehttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/install-azure-cli).

- If you have not already done so, create your VNet and
  Compute instances in Azure (Microsoft Azure). See the
  [Azure documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/) for guidance.

* Have either the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) or
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role in Atlas.

* Have a Google Cloud user account with an IAM (Identity and Access Management) user policy and a
  [Compute Network Admin](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles#compute.networkAdmin)
  role that grants permissions to create, modify, and delete
  networking resources. For more information on managing
  private endpoints and connections, see the [GCP
  documentation](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/overview.html).

* [Install gcloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install).

* If you have not already done so, create your VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) and
  Compute instances in Google Cloud. See the [GCP
  documentation](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/using-vpc.html) for guidance.

* Make sure egress firewall rules permit traffic to the
  internal IP address of the GCP Private Service Connect endpoint.

* (Optional) If you enforce a security perimeter with VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
  service controls (VPC-SC), you must create ingress and
  egress rules to establish the connection between the
  GCP Private Service Connect endpoint and Atlas clusters. See the
  [GCP documentation](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/configure-private-service-connect-services#vpc-sc)
  for guidance.

* Enable [global access](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/about-accessing-vpc-hosted-services-endpoints#global-access)
  to use Private Service Connect to connect to Atlas
  clusters in different regions.
